pattern: Solving a question only ever affects which future questions stay reachable, so scoring the exam from the last question backward turns every decision into a simple choice between two already-known numbers.
hints: Think of this as one decision per question, taken in order, where solving a question changes which future questions are even reachable.

Work from the last question toward the first: once you know the best score obtainable starting at question i+1, question i's best score is just a choice between skipping it or banking its points and jumping past its cooldown.
Store one best-score value per starting index, built back-to-front; the final answer is that array's value at index 0, and jumping past a cooldown should never reach past the end of the exam.

edge_inputs.description: solving a smaller early question can still beat jumping straight to a bigger later one
every cooldown is long enough that only a single question can ever be solved
only one question on the exam
identical maximum-length cooldowns that all tie on points
the last question's cooldown reaches past the end of the exam and must be clamped
the single richest question is a trap -- skipping it unlocks a higher-scoring combination later
a one-question cooldown still lets you take almost every other question by alternating

stress.description: random points/cooldown pairs across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    questions = [[rng.randint(1, 100000), rng.randint(1, 100000)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [questions]
